# globalObject

**Instance Property**

The JavaScript global object associated with the context.

## Declaration

```swift
var globalObject: JSValue! { get }
```

## Discussion

In a web browser, the global object of a JavaScript context is the browser window (the `window` object in JavaScript). Outside of web-browser use, a context’s global object serves a similar role, separating the JavaScript namespaces of different contexts. Global variables within a script appear as fields or subscripts in the global object—you can access them either through this [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue] object or through the methods listed in the Accessing JavaScript global state with subscripts section in [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSContext].



## Working with JavaScript global state

- **exception**: A JavaScript exception to be thrown in evaluation of the script.
- **exceptionHandler**: A block to be invoked should evaluating a script result in a JavaScript exception being thrown.
- **virtualMachine**: The JavaScript virtual machine to which the context belongs.
- **name**: A descriptive name for the context.
